Redstone Line (Corner).best_variant: Explain the construction emphasizing SPATIAL RELATIONSHIPS and SIGNAL PATHS. For each block, say what it connects to.

best_result.overall_plan: The circuit creates a 90-degree signal path starting from a lever, traversing a straight line, turning, and terminating in a lamp. The signal flows from the lever, through a chain of redstone dust, ending at the lamp block which updates based on the wire state.

construction_steps.reasoning: Place the lever at the origin to serve as the power source. This block acts as the input device that initiates the signal flow when toggled.
Place redstone dust at (1,0,0) to immediately receive the redstone signal from the lever at (0,0,0) and transmit it toward the corner segment.
Place redstone dust at (2,0,0) to continue the signal path along the X-axis, bridging the gap between the first wire and the corner point.
Place redstone dust at (3,0,0) to act as the corner pivot, allowing the signal to transition from the X-axis to the Z-axis.
Place redstone dust at (3,0,1) to carry the signal forward along the Z-axis after the corner hub at (3,0,0).
Place redstone dust at (3,0,2) to finalize the signal path extension, positioning it directly adjacent to the lamp at (3,0,3).
Place the redstone lamp at (3,0,3) as the final component. It relies on the redstone wire at (3,0,2) for power; when the wire is powered, the lamp will update to the lit state.

Piston Door (2x2).best_variant: Explain the construction emphasizing SPATIAL RELATIONSHIPS and SIGNAL PATHS. For each block, say what it connects to.

best_result.overall_plan: The goal is to create a 2x2 flush piston door. The system uses a lever at the start to transmit a signal through a line of redstone wire. This signal activates four sticky pistons laid out in a 2x2 grid, which push stone blocks upward to block the passage when powered, or retract them when unpowered.

construction_steps.reasoning: This is the primary input device. It sits at the start of the circuit to provide a togglable power source for the entire mechanism.
This wire connects the lever at (-2,0,0) to the main transmission line at (0,0,0), allowing the signal to travel away from the source.
This wire serves as the central hub of the transmission line, bridging the gap between the input segment and the piston array.
This wire acts as the final link, carrying the signal directly to the adjacent sticky pistons at (2,0,0) and (3,0,0) to trigger them.
Placed adjacent to the redstone wire at (1,0,0) so it receives the power signal. It must face up to push the stone block at (2,1,0) into the doorway.
Placed behind the first piston to form the 2x2 grid. It receives power from the block it is adjacent to and pushes the stone block at (2,1,1).
Placed in the second column of the array. It is powered by the wire at (1,0,0) and drives the stone block at (3,1,0).
The final actuator in the 2x2 array. It sits adjacent to the piston at (3,0,0) and pushes the stone block at (3,1,1).
Placed on top of the sticky piston at (2,0,0) to act as a door surface that moves vertically.
Placed on top of the sticky piston at (2,0,1) to complete the left side of the door.
Placed on top of the sticky piston at (3,0,0) to complete the right side of the door.
Placed on top of the sticky piston at (3,0,1) to finish the 2x2 wall.
